video_description: • 𝗔  𝗡 𝗢 𝗩 𝗘 𝗟  𝗘 𝗩 𝗘 𝗡 𝗜 𝗡 𝗚 •⁣ ⁣ Looking back of the first year of my podcast and I won't lie, I'm pinching myself just a little... I started off with some of my absolute favourite bookstagrammers, and adored chatting nonsense with my pals... And then (with lots of encouragement from said pals) I reached out to authors.⁣ ⁣ Fast forward a year and almost 100 episodes and I still cannot believe the authors who have joined me on the pod... Who are willing to talk silliness with me... and every single guest has been a JOY. ⁣ ⁣ I've gotten to chat to JOHN SILVER HIMSELF @longlukearnold, talk bathtub tequila with @kiran_mh, hit up the m&m store with @susannahdickey, talk Roald Dahl's food with @thebobpalmer, ponder the future of social media with @isaacmarion, fangirl over @_sophiehaydock_, eat cake in an Exeter hotel with @elodielharper, laugh over Devon living with @sarahpearseauthor. I really could go on and on...⁣ and this is just a small selection of those I've welcomed (ya know, after I figured out canva 🤣) ⁣ 𝘚𝘰 𝘢 𝘩𝘶𝘨𝘦 𝘵𝘩𝘢𝘯𝘬 𝘺𝘰𝘶 𝘵𝘰 𝘦𝘷𝘦𝘳𝘺 𝘴𝘪𝘯𝘨𝘭𝘦 𝘱𝘦𝘳𝘴𝘰𝘯 𝘸𝘩𝘰 𝘩𝘢𝘴 𝘫𝘰𝘪𝘯𝘦𝘥 𝘮𝘦, 𝘸𝘩𝘰 𝘩𝘢𝘴 𝘦𝘯𝘤𝘰𝘶𝘳𝘢𝘨𝘦𝘥 𝘮𝘦, 𝘩𝘢𝘴 𝘴𝘩𝘢𝘳𝘦𝘥 𝘦𝘱𝘪𝘴𝘰𝘥𝘦𝘴, 𝘩𝘢𝘴 𝘴𝘦𝘯𝘵 𝘮𝘦 𝘮𝘦𝘴𝘴𝘢𝘨𝘦𝘴 - 𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘴 𝘸𝘢𝘴 𝘢 𝘤𝘳𝘢𝘻𝘺 𝘭𝘪𝘵𝘵𝘭𝘦 𝘪𝘥𝘦𝘢 𝘤𝘰𝘯𝘫𝘶𝘳𝘦𝘥 𝘶𝘱 𝘵𝘩𝘢𝘵 𝘴𝘰𝘮𝘦𝘩𝘰𝘸, 𝘩𝘢𝘴 𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘰𝘸𝘦𝘥 𝘮𝘦 𝘵𝘰 𝘵𝘢𝘭𝘬 𝘵𝘰 𝘱𝘦𝘰𝘱𝘭𝘦 𝘐 𝘢𝘥𝘰𝘳𝘦 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘢𝘥𝘮𝘪𝘳𝘦. ❤️⁣ ⁣ #BookishPodcast #ANovelEvening #BookwormChat #ReadersPodcast #BookishPod #FictionalDinnerParty #BookParty #BiblioPodcast #Bookaholic #BookChat #BookSounds #Podcaster #BookCaster⁣ #BookishCast #LitPodcast #LitReads #Bookworms #BookLovers #BookCommunity

voice_to_text: He sees the inspector to the door and holds out his hand. The man's hand doesn't grasp his. It's limp, lifeless, so that he has to make an effort to shake it to support the hand that's like an amorphous mass, a dead fish. Before turning away, the inspector looks him in the eye and says, this job would be pretty easy if everyone could just sign and not do anything else, don't you think? He doesn't say anything. What he thinks is that it's an impertinence, even if he does understand. He understands the powerlessness felt by this young inspector who needs something out of the ordinary to happen so his day will be worthwhile. This inspector who's suspicious about the whole scene and has to resign himself to not doing his job. This inspector who's clearly not corrupt, who never would have accepted a bribe, who's an honest man because there are a few things he doesn't understand yet. This inspector who reminds him so much of himself when he was young, before the processing plant, the doubts, his baby, the series of daily deaths, and thought that complying with regulations was what mattered most. When in some inaccessible corner of his mind, he was glad about the transition, glad to have this new job, to be a part of this historic change, to be thinking about the rules that people would have to comply with long after he disappeared from the world. Because the regulations, he thought Are my legacy, the Mark I'll leave behind. He never would have imagined he'd break the very law he established.

voice_to_text: It occurs to him that in this case the flesh trade is literal, and he's disgusted. While he thinks about this, he tries to eat the candied vegetables, avoiding the fingers that have been cut into small pieces. He's sitting next to Urlet, and the man looks at him and says into his ear, you have to respect what's being served, cavalier. Every dish contains death. Think of it as a sacrifice that some have made for others. I let snails graze his hand again and he shudders. He thinks he can hear the scratching under the man skin, the contained whale, the presence that wants to get out. He swallows the fresh fingers because he wants this to be over with, to leave as quickly as possible. Or let's. False theories are not something he cares to argue about. He's not going to tell the man that a sacrifice generally requires consent from the person being sacrificed. Nor will he comment that everything contains death, not just this dish, and that he, or let, is also dying with every second that passes, like all of them. To his surprise, the fingers are exquisite. He realizes how much he misses eating meat.

voice_to_text: Tender is the flesh. Do they ever arrive dead? The taller applicant continues. He looks at the man somewhat surprised. Job applicants don't usually ask questions like these, and the fact that this one is doing so intrigues him. If you arrive dead. But every so often we get one. When that happens, the FSA is informed and they come take the head away. He knows that this is the official truth, which makes it a relative truth. He knows because he sees to it that the employees leave a few head for the scavengers, who slaughter the meat with machetes and take what they can. They don't care that they meet is diseased. They run the risk because they can't afford to buy it. He lets it go and tries to see the gesture as an act of charity or perhaps of mercy. But he lets it go as a means of appeasing the scavengers and their hunger. The craving for meat is dangerous.

voice_to_text: How do you write sudden noises in first person POV? Like when someone taps on a car window and it scares the main character, do you describe their bodily reaction? I think onomatopoeia, that is the writing of sounds. So like Bam or like Ding dong. Like those are onomatopoeia. That works to show the kind of sound that was, but it doesn't work the same way as it would if you were watching this in a movie and you suddenly heard the noise. Because think about it, like, nobody reads like Bam and it's like, you know. So I think it's more effective to describe how the main character feels. So like what their reaction was to the noise. And if you've developed this character correctly and your readers care about them, they will empathize. Instead of trying to make your readers feel the same way as a character. Cause you can't startle a reader. Like, there's no jump scares in books. There's of course like it can be scary, but it's like a slow burn scare, you know? The other option, instead of using onomatopoeia to call out the sound, is to describe sound for what it is, not necessarily like replicating it in writing. That way in a single sentence, you get to explain the noise and show how it affects the characters. So for example, you could have something like a blood curdling scream rang out across the field, chilling me to the bone.

voice_to_text: hello and welcome to a little segment that I'm calling a novel review where I'm going to do little bite size reviews of some of my reads they might be from guests who have already featured on the podcast they might be from guests who have already featured on the podcast they might be from guests who have already featured on the podcast they might be from guests who have already featured on the podcast they might be a little hint to guests who are up and coming on the podcast or it might just be books that I've managed to tackle from my humongous TBR pile but we are gonna start with this book boys in the valley by Philip Fracassi I was very lucky to be gifted this by Orbit Books and if you love a good horror I am a notorious wimp but if you want a horror there's a little bit Lord of the flies meets the Exorcist this is the one for you creepy orphanage run by priests landlock in the middle of nowhere surrounded by snow and the devil comes calling the boys are split into factions of the possessed and those fighting for good and it's got Gore it's got slow creeping horror but it's written so so beautifully this is an absolute 5 star book for me and one I would highly recommend checking out even if perhaps like me you're a bit of a wimp

video_description: Type Letters are Molecules is a book that extends into multiple smears of what a book could become or never was. It is an anti-book, a broken book, unfinished like the mystery of a border. If there is an "author" of Type, it is the force between female friendship, between poet-publisher & publisher-poet. It is the act of resonance through care and trust. Letters, gestures, and other inscriptions are both the flesh and vehicle connecting, disrupting and expanding the invisible molecules of asemic affect and its translations."   #booktok #books #handmadebooks #poet #poetry #poetsoftiktok #bookasmr #bookaesthetic #experiment #experimentalpoetry #asmrpaper #sounds #sourpatchpress #soundscapes #papersounds #papercraft #letterpress #letterpressprinting #handmade #toadallyhandmade #asmr #asmrsounds #unveling #bookrelease #smallpress #bookrecommendations #tiktokasmr #art #artist #artistsoftiktok #booksounds #asmr "

voice_to_text: I finished The Daughter of the Pirate King let's talk about it this book was a journey to go on it is ya so that made it a little bit easier for me to read honestly I just got done with a guitar so a 300 page pirate book like I ate it up it has been so much fun the chapters are kind of a little bit long but I still read this in 48 hours if you just like put your mind to it it goes by fast 20 page chapters can sometimes put me in a slump but this one didn't so this is about a Loosa daughter of the Pirate King she allows herself to get captured by one of the enemy pirate boat cruise in order to find one part of this ancient map that's going to leave them to the island of mermaids the crew that captures her is a younger crew and it's ran by 2 brothers who their father had just passed away there's immediately sexual tension with someone on board so there is romance there are romance scenes in this there's not really spice so if you're not into that then this is great for you if you are into that then this doesn't really have much in it but it's still so fun I don't read a lot of underwater pirate types of books so I didn't really know what to expect we learn a lot about Eloza so I end up really enjoying her um we learn about crew members that are on some are really nice so it wasn't like hardcore fantasy where you know she's taken aboard a ship and she's treated like so awful it's really mild but I liked that I enjoyed it I enjoyed my time reading this it was fun I thought that I would be able to predict everything honestly I was like uh I guess I would probably see a movie like this on Disney Channel or something but I really couldn't like when it got to the end I'm like oh that was a twist like that was interesting that was a twist it was just it's so fun I'm going to read the next book I've heard that daughter of the Siren Queen is better to doology so we'll see but it was just it was a fun read if you're looking for something just fun and different for your summer that or The Pirate King I would recommend why not already on Goodreads like 3.5 stars but fun really fun have I said that enough

voice_to_text: for this novel review I'm gonna be talking about book that I know is so beloved and this might be a bit of a controversial one but I'm gonna be talking about Wolf Song by TJ Klune okay positives first despite how chunky is it's very well written it's very well written it's very easy to read it's very easy to read I do love the magic in this um and I love the setting I do think that the writing is so vivid and brings its life however and some people will not agree with this for me the age gap situation was a little hard to overcome and I did find myself finding a little bit dick with the main characters I don't wanna give away any spoilers for anyone who hasn't read this um so I'm not gonna dive too deep into this but for me when you've known someone since they were literally a child and you're a teenager it doesn't work for me but there are lots of pluses to this book it is an excellent piece of fantasy in terms it's writing and how easy it is to get into but for me I really didn't connect that well with the characters either there was some bits that just felt I know they've been tried to me it was a little weird how cool everyone was with werewolves just existing um so that's why I'm giving this three and a/2 stars

voice_to_text: for this week's current read I've been diving into some proper historical fiction one of my favourite authors ever Alison Wear and this is Elizabeth of York the last White Rose um I'm loving it this is obviously part of the whole Queen series that she's done she's done all of Henry VIII Queens uh from their perspectives now we're looking at the last princess of York and it's so good it is chunky it is a chunky book I wouldn't say there's anything so far that's kind of new or groundbreaking you'll have read this story before but I love reading Elizabeth's point of view um and I do love a good historical fiction so if you enjoy Alison's work or you wanna dive into history this is a good one to start with

voice_to_text: for this novel review for this novel review I will be talking all about Sea Change by Gina Chung who I was lucky enough to welcome onto the podcast to talk all about this extraordinary book firstly it's the most gorgeous proof that I think I possibly own and it is the most beautiful story all about loss all about grief and all about relationships be it with friends be it with lovers and sometimes with a giant octopus named Dolores I would highly recommend checking out this beautiful novel and also checking out Gina's episode of a Novel Evening where we talk all about the inspirations behind this stunning novel that was absolute solid 5 stars from me

voice_to_text: for this novel review I'm gonna be looking at my Best Friend's Exorcism by Grady Hendrix I'm such a huge fan of Hendrix's writing and obviously this one has been made into a show on Netflix I haven't been brave enough to check it out yet but I finally got stuck into this and oh my goodness this is an 80s nostalgia trip it is so fun but also really scary really gory and I can absolutely see why this was made for the screen it is so so visual it gives me like Heather's vibes a little bit it's got a bit of that going on in there and I absolutely I read this in a day and I cannot recommend it enough 5 out of five

voice_to_text: Come riconoscere l'epoca di un libro dal suono che fa esatto? Ogni libro emette un suono diverso sfogliandolo e ovviamente dovuto alla carta da cui è composto. Oggi vediamo come diverse tipologie di carta quindi emettono suoni diversi. Le prime carte Dalla fine del 400 e per tutto il 500 sono molto durature ma il loro spessore non eccessivo, quindi sfogliandole si sentono un suono armonico e delicato nel 600, nel 700 le carte sono ancora di ottima fattura e forte consistenza, suonando in maniera un Po legnosa, direi quasi croccante. Tra il l'ottocento e il 900 la produzione diventa massiva e quindi metodi sempre più aggiornati, il libro comincia a suonare un Po più freddamente, senza quei tipici scricchioli della carta.

voice_to_text: so I thought I'd share with you what I'm reading this week because I'm actually breaking not a rule but I'm breaking what I usually do and I'm usually a one book at a time kind of person um but I'm reading three I don't know why I've just decided to do it this way so gonna show you what I'm reading so starting off with my non fiction I've gone for Divine Might oh I've covered over that Divine Might by Natalie Haynes I'm reading this a little slower cause it is non fiction it's about the Greek goddesses I'm finding it a little slow I will get there in the end but I'm reading like a little bit now and again when I just need little like non fiction break and then I am reading rule of Wolves um again I haven't my shadow and bone I have googled what happened though but I did enjoy king of scars once a bit um so I've dived into this one while it's fresh I've barely read anything so this is a short start and then I know this is well I'm reading Lilith I mean look at that it's beautiful um again not very far on these it's not very impressive is it but I'm really fascinated by Lilith as a character anyway um and this is all clues to an upcoming episode of a novel evening that's coming up um it's really unusual read not like anything I've read before um I'm not gonna say much more than that cause I've barely read any um but it's nice short chapters that means that I can read a little short chapter of this and then grab something else and get through a little short chapter that but it's a good mixed bag so I will keep you posted on how I'm getting on with this

voice_to_text: okay here's another little novel review for you and this time it is of her radiant curse by Elizabeth Lim and this time it is of her radiant curse by Elizabeth Lim I was not even aware that this is kind of a prologue um to Dragon's promise um and the geology there but I absolutely love this um it's so much fun it's so good it's got one beautiful sister with a hidden secret and one cursed sister um who's known as the Green Snake Lady um absolutely this is one of you love a good fantasy that's rooted in Asian folklore has such amazing well building is just it's it writes women so so well in this story um Elizabeth Lynn is great at creating these female characters who are like conflicted who are strong but still feminine this is one for you I absolutely loved it I like devoured it in a day get it get on it also matches my walls beautifully matches my room um so absolutely check this one out

voice_to_text: for my little mini review for this week I know it's been out for ages but I'm going to be checking out two books um despite having never read uh Shadow Line I was gifted these for my birthday by a lovely pal a pair of lovely pals actually cause they married um considering I went into this pretty blind having only watched the first season of the show I really enjoyed these um for anyone who has watched Shadow Bone it probably really interesting cause you know all the characters you know exactly what's going on so even more everything and once I kind of found my place in the world I thought these were a really interesting kind of expansion to the grishaverse I thought that the action it was really cool there was some really cool magic yeah there were points where I was super confused because I didn't know who anyone was or where I was or what was happening but that aside thoroughly enjoyed these two so if you like a bit of action magic bit of romance don't give anything away but if you like a bit of romance you didn't see coming there's also some deaths I didn't think would happen which we love so absolutely check these bad boys out

voice_to_text: probably the only thing I love more than reading is music and so when I read a book that brings both of those together it is some of my happiest moments Sumary Kami has been one of my favorite writers for years and years now and I could never quite figure out what about his writing is quite so hypnotic was brings you into his stories um until I've reread at Norwegian Wood it was the first one I came across 10 years ago when I was still living in Paris it's a beautiful sad book about memory and about living life to the fullest and it covers a lot of different ground definitely was a bit confusing for me when I first read the book I mean there's not a clear plot delineation in fact there's not even really a true scenes in the way there aren't a lot of action sequences there's certainly no stakes it kind of throws the rule book out about what makes good writing most of the time but there is this kind of hypnotic pull underneath that I think can be described more like music and the clue is really in the title as I read through it there's this deep kind of mournful almost funerial push to the writing as it's only when you think about the song Region Wood that this kind of slow mournful pull starts to make sense like it is a song about loss it is a song about the this mysterious woman that comes through your life and you're unable to ever quite shake that she's not there anymore she still seems to inhabit your whole life and I think that's really what this book is about but it all it is also the way the book is written this a set of for Shinjuku to kill some time while walking the streets the Sunday crowds gave me some relief The Kinokunii Bookshop was as jam packed as a rush hour train I brought a copy of Falconers Light in August and went to the noisiest cafe I could think of reading my new book while listening to the Ornet Coleman and Bud Powell and drinking hot thick foul tasting coffee at 5:30 I closed my book went outside and ate a light supper how many Sundays how many hundreds of Sundays like this lay ahead of me quiet peaceful and lonely I said aloud to myself on Sundays I didn't wind my spring this isn't what normally counts as good writing it's not the kind of writing that you will get taught in any MFA or writing school that way but it is so loaded with symbolism it's so simple and elegant and graceful but most importantly it's so very rhythmic in that way a song like Norwegian Wood is that it is absolutely unputdownable

video_description: Thought this would be a good lil sound to use so you guys can learn new lil bits about me!  I have SO MANY NICKNAMES🤪 My real name is Ellie-Mae but most either call me, Ellie, Doris or Smells! Smelly Ellie here🫶🏽 I am from Bristol (UK girlie)🌎 Favourite genre📖 EVERYTHING Romance! Fantasy & dark romance are my huge main two💘 Tomorrow is my ONE month anniversary being on tiktok as my book page!! I am so so grateful for you all🥹🥹 Which way do I prefer reading? I love both! BUT I love my kindle, as you can see.. it’s really my pride & joy but physical books will always be special for me because that is where it all started for me☺️ Do I have a kindle? SILLY QUESTION GIRLIE OF COURSE I DO!! Best thing I ever purchased🕺🏼 the amount of money I have saved, the amount of books I have read😚 I have never really been a library girl?? Do people still do this?! I wouldn’t even know how it’s work currently!😂 ONE BOOK! I struggle to read more than one, I have done it twice but it has to be a different physical book AND a Kindle book for sure!  Current read - see you soon by Lexie Axelson! If you are still here reading.. thank you for sticking around & showing the love! Love ya all!💘 #bookrecommendations #booksounds #bookcover #kindleunlimited #kindle #kindlestickersappreciationpost #cowboybooks #fantasty #darkromance #darkromancebooksoftiktok #booknicknames #doris #smellyellie #readingonebookatatime

voice_to_text: Welcome to my little book shelf. I absolutely love the way books sound and I think this trigger is slightly underrated. If you do not like whispering, I have a non whispered version of this right before this post. But if you do like whispering, let me know your thoughts down below. Since this is my first ever whispered ASMR our video, I can't decide if I like the scratching on the books better or if I like the topping better to find together. I didn't realize it would take this long to do some whispering. So that is it.

video_description: Countdown läuft Am 10.10.2024 erscheint Drewmore Dynasty Teil eins der Trilogie. Dark Romance trifft auf Academy! Ihr wollt wissen worum es in dem Buch geht?  Klappentext: Eine Universität, an die ich nie wollte. Machenschaften, von denen ich nichts wissen sollte. Und ein Sommer, den ich niemals so erwartet hätte. Zwei Elite-Unis, die Schicksal spielen. Vier Männer, deren Absichten im Verborgenen liegen, und eine Studentin auf der Suche nach der Wahrheit. Zwischen mysteriösen Vermisstenfällen, rätselhaften Morden und verbotenen Romanzen stellt sich die Frage: Was verbirgt sich hinter den geheimnisvollen Machenschaften an den Unis und wird Vivien es schaffen, ihre Schwester zu retten? Vivien van Jones ist eine junge Frau, die weiß, was sie will, und die sich nimmt, wonach ihr der Sinn steht. Auch ihre Wirkung auf Männer ist ihr durchaus bewusst. Dabei hat sie nur ein Ziel: mit ihrer Schwester Lexy nach dem gemeinsamen Medizinstudium eine Arztpraxis eröffnen. Doch Viviens exzessives Verhalten ist ihrem Vater, einem bekannten Nobelpreisträger, ein Dorn im Auge und so beschließt er kurzerhand, beide Töchter an verschiedenen Elite-Unis anzumelden. Kaum an der Uni angekommen, trifft Vivien auf den charmanten Valentin, dessen geheimnisvolle Ausstrahlung sie magisch anzieht. Anders sieht es bei Aurel aus, dem sie versucht, aus dem Weg zu gehen. Nachdem mehrfach Studenten auf mysteriöse Weise verschwinden, kann Vivien ihre Schwester Lexy an deren Uni in Zürich nicht mehr erreichen. Warum meldet sie sich nicht? Und was hat der gut aussehende Dekan Chalier mit der ganzen Sache zu tun? »An dieser Universität geschehen so einige Dinge, aber mit Sicherheit werden hier keine Gentlemen ausgebildet, das kann ich dir versprechen.« Buch: Drewmore Dynasty Erscheint am 10.10.2024 Autorin @KariTenero  Verlag @Black Edition  Werbung {Bloggerteam} #darkromance #darkromancebooks #book #tiktok #bookblogger #viral #fy #fyp #fypage #sound #booksounds #blackedition #booktoker

voice_to_text: You know those first few minutes after you finished an amazing book? Those moments when you aren't quite sure what to do with yourself? You simply sit there staring at the last words. Unsure how to move on with your life. How can it be over? How can those characters just fade to black for you? The characters are still imprinted on your soul. Their actions, their dialogue, still alive and strong in your mind. I love that feeling.

“Why didn’t you tell me, Wes?” I heard myself ask, my voice cracking. 

“What?” He narrowed his eyes. “Tell you what?” 

“Why didn’t you tell me about everything you were dealing with after your dad died?” I asked, abandoning any hope of keeping it together. voice_to_text: Who is he? My ex boyfriend. Your ex boyfriend? Yes. Why is your ex boyfriend sending you roses and love notes? It's not a love note. It damn well looks like one to me. What does he mean by change your mind? I told him about our engagement a few months ago. He showed up in New York and implied he'd be open to giving our relationship another shot. I declined. He left. The end. Obviously not the end, given this lovely bouquet he sent you. It's just flowers. They're harmless. Some fucker is sending you flowers and you wanna tell me it's harmless? He picked up the card again. Thought of you at midnight. Hope you're doing well. Love, Heath. It doesn't take a genius to know what he was doing while he was thinking of you at midnight.
